---
title: "Zooplankton QA Report"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(targets)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
tar_load(
  clean_data,
  qa_summary,
  sample_summary,
  missing_required,
  negative_counts,
  nonpositive_split_factor,
  unexpected_sex_values,
  duplicate_organism_rows,
  station_standardization,
  qa_link_issues
)
```

## Overview

```{r}
data.frame(
  n_rows = nrow(clean_data),
  n_samples = dplyr::n_distinct(clean_data$sample_num),
  n_source_files = dplyr::n_distinct(clean_data$source_file),
  n_taxa = dplyr::n_distinct(clean_data$species_name)
) |>
  knitr::kable()
```

## QA summary

```{r}
qa_summary |>
  knitr::kable()
```

## Sample summary

```{r}
sample_summary |>
  arrange(source_file, sample_num) |>
  knitr::kable()
```

## Missing required values

```{r}
missing_required |>
  head(50) |>
  knitr::kable()
```

## Negative counts

```{r}
negative_counts |>
  head(50) |>
  knitr::kable()
```

## Nonpositive split factor

```{r}
nonpositive_split_factor |>
  head(50) |>
  knitr::kable()
```

## Unexpected sex values

```{r}
unexpected_sex_values |>
  head(50) |>
  knitr::kable()
```

## Duplicate organism rows

```{r}
duplicate_organism_rows |>
  head(50) |>
  knitr::kable()
```

## Station standardization

```{r}
station_standardization |>
  head(50) |>
  knitr::kable()
```

## QA link issues

```{r}
qa_link_issues |>
  head(50) |>
  knitr::kable()
```
